title: 5 Social Media Mistakes Small Businesses Make (And How to Fix Them)
author: Michelle Lin
excerpt: Most small businesses aren't failing at social media because of bad content. They're failing because of bad strategy. Here are the five mistakes we see most — and the fixes that actually work.
body:
## It's Not a Content Problem

When small businesses come to us frustrated with social media, they almost always say the same thing: "We're posting, but nothing's happening." The content is usually fine. The strategy is where things break.

Here are the five mistakes we see most often — and exactly how to fix them.

## Mistake 1: Posting Without a Content Pillar System

If every post is a product shot, you're boring your audience. If every post is a meme, you're entertaining but not converting. You need 3-4 content pillars that balance brand building with sales.

**Fix:** Define your pillars. For most small businesses, we recommend: Product (30%), Education (30%), Behind-the-Scenes (25%), Community/UGC (15%).

## Mistake 2: Ignoring Stories and DMs

The feed is your shopfront. Stories and DMs are where relationships happen. Most small businesses post to the feed and ignore everything else.

**Fix:** Post 3-5 Stories per day. Reply to every DM within 2 hours. Use polls, questions, and quizzes to drive interaction.

## Mistake 3: Chasing Vanity Metrics

Follower count means nothing if those followers aren't buying. We've seen accounts with 50K followers and zero sales, and accounts with 3K followers doing $20K months.

**Fix:** Track metrics that matter: saves, shares, profile visits, link clicks, and DM enquiries. These are buying signals.

## Mistake 4: No Call to Action

Every post needs a job. If you don't tell people what to do, they'll do nothing.

**Fix:** End every post with a clear CTA: "Save this for later," "Share with a friend who needs this," "Link in bio to shop," or "DM us 'MENU' for our full list."

## Mistake 5: Trying to Be Everywhere

You don't need to be on Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, LinkedIn, Pinterest, and YouTube. You need to be excellent on one or two platforms where your customers actually spend time.

**Fix:** Pick your primary platform based on where your audience is. Go deep. Master it. Only expand when you've built a sustainable rhythm.

## The Takeaway

Social media works for small businesses — but only with the right strategy underneath it. Fix these five things and you'll see results within 30 days.
